name: trigger_event
description: Trigger an event on one or more Pusher channels. All clients subscribed to the specified channel(s) will receive the event data in real time via their WebSocket connection. The event name should follow a naming convention (e.g., 'order-updated', 'new-message'). The data payload is serialized as JSON and limited to 10KB. You can target a single channel or up to 100 channels in one request. Optionally exclude a specific socket_id from receiving the event (useful to avoid echo for the triggering client).

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: Your Pusher application ID. This is a path parameter in the URL.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The event name (e.g., 'new-message', 'order-updated'). Client-side listeners subscribe to events by name. Max 200 characters.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The event payload as a JSON-encoded string. Maximum 10KB. This is what connected clients receive when the event fires.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: The channel to trigger the event on (e.g., 'orders', 'private-user-123', 'presence-room-42'). Use this for a single channel. Either 'channel' or 'channels' must be provided.

parameters.name: channels
parameters.description: Array of channel names to trigger the event on (max 100). Use this to broadcast to multiple channels at once. Either 'channel' or 'channels' must be provided.

parameters.name: socket_id
parameters.description: A socket ID to exclude from receiving the event. Used to prevent the triggering client from receiving its own event (anti-echo). Format: '123456.789012'.
